grant_title: San Bernardino County 2026-27 Annual Action Plan for Homelessness, Housing, and Community Development
geographic_restrictions: Unincorporated areas of San Bernardino County and 13 participating cities (Adelanto, Barstow, Big Bear Lake, Colton, Grand Terrace, Highland, Loma Linda, Montclair, Needles, Redlands, Twentynine Palms, Yucaipa, and Yucca Valley). Chino Hills and Rancho Cucamonga participate only in the HOME program.
project_area: Homelessness response, affordable housing construction, infrastructure improvements, and community development services for low- and moderate-income residents
demographic_requirements: Priority given to low- and very low-income households, transitional-age youth (TAY), and unsheltered individuals. Funds target Americans with Disabilities Act-compliant infrastructure, senior meal programs, and evidence-based homelessness interventions.
eligibility_summary: Funds are allocated through partnerships with qualified housing developers, nonprofit organizations, and county departments. Eligible projects include affordable housing construction, homelessness prevention, rapid rehousing, emergency shelter services, street outreach, and infrastructure improvements. Applicants must align with the county's 2022 Homeless Strategic Action Plan and demonstrate measurable outcomes in reducing unsheltered homelessness and improving housing stability.
